_about: spck.dev site claims register — every factual claim on the site that is not data-rendered from coverage/agent-coverage JSON must be registered here with evidence and a review-by date, or removed. Bootstrapped by ops/site-content-audit-2026-07.md (Phase 0 of the 2026-07-09 IA redesign). The manifest block is the bindable public source for product counts that do NOT exist in coverage JSONs (which count MUST rows, not checks); the site-freshness gate regenerates these numbers from the engine and goes RED on drift.

claims.text: 193 kill-rate-validated checks, live coverage matrix, CLI + CI + web, multi-version support.
claims.evidence: conformance/coverage/coverage_gate.py copy-freshness gate counts MCheck( registrations across conformance/checks/merchant_checks*.py = 193 (verified 2026-07-09) and pins this exact phrase via regex; bind number to site_claims.json:$.manifest.merchant_checks

Merchant-side checks hero stat — gate-enforced by coverage_gate.py stat-num regex; NOT in coverage.json (its check fields count MUST rows 117/125/234); data-live bind to site_claims.json:$.manifest.merchant_checks

claims.text: Every check is proven to catch its own bug (kill-rate validated).
claims.evidence: conformance/selfcheck/mutation_killrate.py + conformance/selfcheck/validate_merchant_checks.py — run_suite gates 'merchant' and 'killrate' block release if any check fails to kill its injected defect

claims.text: every check is proven to catch the bug it’s for, so there are no false greens
claims.evidence: conformance/selfcheck/mutation_killrate.py + validate_merchant_checks.py (merchant lane) and conformance/agent/run_agent.py reference gate (agent lane): clean-pass AND kill required per check

'False greens' hero stat — kill-rate mechanics: a check that survives its targeted defect is flagged UNSAFE and blocks release (mutation_killrate.py; validate_merchant_checks.py; run_agent.py reference gate)

Agent-side checks hero stat — conformance/agent/agent_governance.py _agent_copy_freshness() enforces len(agent_checks.CHECKS)=42 (verified live import 2026-07-09); NOT in agent-coverage.json (its check=53 counts covered MUST rows); data-live bind to site_claims.json:$.manifest.agent_checks

claims.text: Every normative MUST across three spec versions is accounted for
claims.evidence: conformance/selfcheck/verify_register_completeness.py (denominator complete) + conformance/coverage/coverage_gate.py; every public/coverage.json row carries status check/exempt/gap; 'three' should render from coverage.json:$.spec_pins key count

claims.text: 193 checks, from the browser or your CI
claims.evidence: coverage_gate.py copy-freshness regex '(\d+)\+? checks, from the browser' pins this to the real MCheck count (193, verified 2026-07-09)

claims.text: regenerated with every change and enforced by CI (stale data or a coverage regression fails the build)
claims.evidence: conformance/coverage/coverage_gate.py freshness comparison + conformance/coverage/ratchet.json accounted-coverage floors

claims.text: Roadmap to 100%
claims.evidence: docs/ROADMAP.md (link target exists, verified 2026-07-09)

claims.text: Released January 11, 2026

claims.text: Anything it can't test is reported

claims.evidence: conformance/selfcheck/mutation_killrate.py (incl. the deliberate noop-check canary proving the harness works)

claims.text: VERDICT: INCOMPLETE — 21/98 applicable MUSTs (21%), 0 deviations

claims.evidence: packaging/pyproject.toml L21 dependencies = ["certifi>=2023.7.22"] — exactly one runtime dependency

claims.text: Run the full suite (193 kill-rate-validated checks) from your shell or CI.
claims.evidence: Gate-pinned 193 (coverage_gate.py). PAGE RETIRING (Task 11): claim must not silently migrate — /check already carries the registered equivalent (CLAIM-024)

claims.text: Reports kept for 90 days.
claims.evidence: functions/api/[[path]].js L139 expirationTtl: 7776000 (= 90 days). PAGE RETIRING: saved-reports UI leaves the public surface; KV backend untouched (admin retrieval remains). Do not re-add to public pages without re-registering

claims.evidence: clean-pass + 100% kill on a known-good reference: conformance/selfcheck/validate_merchant_checks.py + conformance/ci/differential.py (independent official Flower Shop sample) + conformance/SOURCES.lock.json pins

claims.evidence: conformance/coverage/exemptions.json + validate_exemptions() in coverage_gate.py (machine-checked against double-booking)

claims.evidence: MCP transport checks exist (conformance/checks/merchant.py + engine.py + merchant_checks.py); version ids ↔ coverage.json:$.spec_pins (LIVE-able). PAGE RETIRING → carry to docs.html; render version list from spec_pins

claims.text: each check is proven to catch the defect it's for (kill-rate testing)
claims.evidence: mutation_killrate.py + conformance/.vendor/ucp-schema oracle anchoring + per-check register citations. PAGE RETIRING → carry to docs.html

claims.text: reports anything it can't test as not-tested rather than a silent pass
claims.evidence: conformance/checks/merchant.py capability scoping. PAGE RETIRING → carry to docs.html

claims.text: 30 seconds, no install — discovery + profile + read-only catalog.
claims.evidence: functions/api/conformance.js — instant-check scope is discovery + profile-structure checks + read-only catalog probes (catalogChecks() runs only search/lookup, 'the ONLY live checks safe to run anonymously online'); '30 seconds' is a UX estimate the claims gate excludes via its seconds allowlist

claims.text: We fetch your store’s /.well-known/ucp and run the discovery + profile-structure checks, plus read-only catalog probes when your store supports them.
claims.evidence: functions/api/conformance.js — fetches /.well-known/ucp server-side, runs discovery/profile-structure checks; catalogChecks() runs read-only catalog search/lookup probes only when the profile declares dev.ucp.shopping.catalog.search

claims.text: Run it only against your own store, never someone else’s.
claims.evidence: Write-path safety instruction for the CLI depth-ladder rung: the full suite exercises checkout/cart/order write paths (conformance/checks/merchant_checks*.py; packaging/spck_conformance/), which must not be pointed at third-party stores — same honesty line as CLAIM-026

claims.text: Every check is proven to catch the defect it’s for — the suite injects that exact bug, and a check that misses it blocks release.
claims.evidence: conformance/selfcheck/mutation_killrate.py + conformance/selfcheck/validate_merchant_checks.py — run_suite 'killrate' and 'merchant' gates fail the build when any check survives its injected defect

claims.text: RFC 9421 request signing
claims.evidence: conformance/agent/agent_checks.py — agent-lane RFC 9421 request-signing checks (SIG-001 et al.; parses the Signature-Input covered components, verified by live import 2026-07-09). Merchant-side RFC 9421 signature checks also live in conformance/checks/merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py. The number 9421 is a normative RFC reference on the coverage agent-lane blurb, not a product metric.

claims._note: B4 scratch (Task 11). Do NOT edit public/site_claims.json directly (race-safe). Trunk should fold these into site_claims.json:$.claims with page=docs.html. NOTE: docs.html was authored to produce ZERO site-claims-gate candidates (verified: no proof-word sentence, no non-excluded number adjacent to a claim-noun), so the claims gate is GREEN on docs.html WITHOUT these entries. They register the genuine capability claims carried from the retired guide.html so the copy stays evidence-backed if the gate ever tightens.

claims.text: reports anything it can't reach as not-tested rather than a silent pass
claims.evidence: conformance/checks/merchant.py capability scoping + not-tested reporting (carries guide.html CLAIM-045 to docs.html)

claims.text: Each check is anchored to the official ucp-schema validator and cites the spec clause it enforces.
claims.evidence: conformance/.vendor/ucp-schema oracle anchoring + per-check register citations (conformance/checks/merchant.py req_id->source mapping); carries guide.html CLAIM-044 to docs.html

claims.text: Each agent check passes a correct agent and catches a broken one.
claims.evidence: conformance/agent/run_agent.py reference gate: every agent check clean-passes the reference agent AND kills its targeted defect (live-verified 2026-07-09); same evidence as agent.html CLAIM-032/CLAIM-035

claims.text: Tests run against the pinned UCP spec versions
claims.evidence: public/coverage.json:$.spec_pins (3 pins: 2026-01-11, 2026-01-23, 2026-04-08); version-date ids are excluded by the claims-gate date allowlist, listed here for freshness traceability; carries guide.html CLAIM-043

claims.text: It fails the build on a MUST deviation and writes a JUnit report.
claims.evidence: action.yml (junit input; exit-code semantics fail the build on any MUST deviation); same evidence as index.html CLAIM-023

claims.text: This page is the published rubric behind every spck.dev result
claims.evidence: verdict semantics implemented in conformance/selfcheck/verdict_gate.py + conformance/checks/merchant.py; rubric ships in the same repo/deploy as the suite

claims.text: the same check must both clean-pass a known-good reference server and flag every defect injected for it
claims.evidence: conformance/checks/engine.py run_check kill_safe (clean==CLEAN and zero surviving mutants) + selfcheck/validate_merchant_checks.py reference gate

claims.text: The report shows the requirement text, its citation into the pinned spec source, and the actual response observed
claims.evidence: merchant.py deviation output: requirement + source + observed body ('each shows expected vs the observed response', merchant.py ~L381)

claims.text: Inconclusive is never counted as a pass
claims.evidence: selfcheck/verdict_gate.py no-false-green semantics: non-kill_safe checks downgrade to INCONCLUSIVE and are excluded from pass

claims.text: Reported explicitly so absence of a failure is never mistaken for a pass
claims.evidence: merchant.py report lists not-tested/not-applicable buckets explicitly; cli.py docstring: reports anything unreachable as not-tested

claims.text: Only a MUST deviation fails the run; the handful of SHOULD checks that run are reported and never fail it
claims.evidence: merchant.py L320: exit 2 iff any MUST deviation; SHOULD MChecks exist (merchant_checks_04_08_oauth.py IDL-043, ORD-025) and do not affect exit code

claims.text: Every check cites a requirement row scraped from the UCP specification, quote-verified against SHA-pinned spec sources
claims.evidence: selfcheck/verify_register.py quote-check (953/953 verified in CI) against conformance/SOURCES.lock.json pinned SHAs

claims.text: check register lists every covered requirement with its citation
claims.evidence: site_gates.py checkdocs gate: page set must equal covered (status=check) register ids, regenerated + byte-compared each run

claims.text: it has to clean-pass a known-good reference implementation and catch each defect injected for it
claims.evidence: same evidence as CLAIM-RUB-002 (engine kill_safe + reference gate)

claims.text: A pass reflects only the checks that ran
claims.evidence: merchant.py verdict line prints validated/applicable MUST counts + explicit not-tested listing; junit records skipped

claims.text: The report says how many applicable MUSTs were validated
claims.evidence: merchant.py VERDICT line: 'N/M applicable MUSTs (x%)' — observed empirically 2026-07-15

claims.text: exit 0 when every applicable MUST that ran passed, exit 2 on any MUST deviation, exit 1 when the target could not be tested at all
claims.evidence: cli.py docstring + merchant.py L320; verified empirically 2026-07-15: golden run exit 2, junk server exit 1, agent lane exit 0

claims.text: The JUnit report names every entry with its register check id; deviations carry the full spec citation
claims.evidence: verified 2026-07-15 parsing a real junit: 194 entries named '<check> (IDS)'; the 4 failure entries embed ucp:docs citations

claims.text: The run verdict is computed over the RFC-2119 MUST requirements applicable to your server
claims.evidence: merchant.py L13: verdict denominator = applicable testable MUSTs (extensions a server doesn't declare drop out)

claims.text: Checks are validated in both directions.
claims.evidence: engine.py run_check: clean-pass on the golden AND every injected mutant caught (kill_safe), else downgrade — same evidence as CLAIM-RUB-002

claims.text: Check register — every covered requirement, spec-cited
claims.evidence: site_gates.py checkdocs gate asserts the /checks page set equals the covered (status=check) register ids and every page carries its pinned citation — same evidence as CLAIM-RUB-008
